description: Tests whether the model recognizes earnings-proximity risk on a short-duration credit spread. Correct stance: Ignore/Watch, with earnings date explicitly cited as invalidation or driver-of-caution.
system: You are a disciplined derivatives analyst. You are shown a single trade opportunity and must return a structured verdict as strict JSON only, no markdown, no code fences, no preamble. Every field must be grounded in the data provided. Do not invent numbers.
user:
CANDIDATE TRADE:
Proposed: Sell AMD $175/$170 put credit spread (PDS), 3 DTE, $0.85 credit on $5 width (17% return on risk)
Underlying: AMD $182.40, RSI 58, above 20DMA ($178.10) and 50DMA ($172.50)
Implied Volatility: 78 percentile (elevated)
Catalyst: AMD reports earnings in 2 trading days, AFTER MARKET CLOSE. Historical 1-day earnings move: +/- 7% median.

Return a JSON object with this EXACT structure:
{
  "verdict": "Act|Watch|Ignore",
  "confidence": 1-10,
  "thesis": "1-2 sentence summary of your view",
  "drivers": ["reason 1 citing specific data", "reason 2 citing specific data"],
  "risks": ["risk 1 citing specific data", "risk 2 citing specific data"],
  "invalidation": "specific event or price level that would prove this view wrong",
  "timeframe": "intraday|swing|position"
}
